note: Conformance ground truth, generated from the JS reference engine. Any RhetorLint engine MUST reproduce every case exactly.

cases.input: We take your privacy extremely seriously, and regrettably, mistakes were made. We are reaching out to affected users.
cases.strip: We take your privacy seriously, and regrettably, mistakes [who?] were made. We are reaching out to affected users.

marks.actual: We take your privacy extremely seriously
marks.note: rehearsed contrition — the shape of an apology with the substance removed
expected: (say the specific thing you did wrong and the specific thing you will do)

marks.note: an intensifier — turns up the emotional volume without adding fact
expected: (state the fact without the intensifier)

marks.note: an agentless passive — who acted is deleted from the sentence
expected: (name who did it: 'I/we + verb')

marks.note: a hedge — lowers a promise you can later deny
expected: (say the concrete commitment, or drop the claim)

cases.input: Everyone knows the plan is working, but what about their failures? Studies show we are the best.
cases.strip: Everyone knows the plan is working, but what about their failures? Studies show we are the best.

marks.note: an absolute — claims more than anyone could actually know
expected: (qualify: how often, who exactly, how certain)

marks.note: a deflection — points away from the question just asked
expected: (answer the original question first)

marks.note: an unnamed authority — 'who says?' is deleted (a named source would instead be SemEval's Appeal to Authority, under Justification)
expected: (name the source, or drop the appeal)

cases.input: I made a mistake and I will fix it by Friday.
cases.strip: I made a mistake and I will fix it by Friday.

cases.input: The report was written by the committee.
cases.strip: The report was written by the committee.

cases.input: We were tired and we were committed to the plan.
cases.strip: We were tired and we were committed to the plan.

cases.input: The system might have possibly failed, allegedly.

marks.note: a deniable modal — leaves room to say you never promised
expected: (state what you know, and what you don't)

marks.note: a deniable modal — leaves room to say you never promised
expected: (state what you know, and what you don't)

expected: (name who did it: 'I/we + verb')

expected: (name who did it: 'I/we + verb')

cases.input: ACT NOW: this offer is gone forever. Limited time, absolutely free, no strings attached.
cases.strip: ACT NOW: this offer is gone forever. Limited time, absolutely free, no strings attached.

marks.note: manufactured urgency — pressure to act before you can weigh it
expected: (state the real deadline and what actually happens after it, or drop the clock)

marks.note: sustained ALL-CAPS — volume standing in for argument
expected: (make the point in sentence case; let the facts carry the emphasis)
